description: To grasp the meaning, significance, or nature of something—to move from confusion to clarity, from outside to inside a concept. 'Shelomui' names the moment when scattered information coheres into comprehension, when what was opaque becomes transparent. It is knowing that includes relationship, not mere data acquisition.
sound_symbolism: The word unfolds gradually, as understanding itself does. 'She' /ʃe/ begins softly, approaching gently. Then 'lo' flows as mind reaches toward meaning. 'Mu' turns inward with nasal depth, integrating new knowledge with existing understanding. Finally 'i' rises bright, the light of comprehension dawning.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi themo shelomui' (I understand something). Can take clausal complements: 'mi shelomui [clause]' (I understand that...). Distinct from sena (know) which implies established knowledge; shelomui emphasizes the process of coming to understand.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Right understanding (samma ditthi) is the first factor of the eightfold path. True comprehension sees things as they are, including impermanence and interdependence.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Understanding precedes peaceful resolution. To understand another's perspective—not just hear it—is the foundation of dialogue.
